title: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
summary: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.

title: How Iran Gained Leverage in the War
summary: Outmatched militarily, Iran used “triangular coercion” by attacking Gulf states and closing the Strait of Hormuz. It points to a long-term U.S. vulnerability.

title: Senate Votes to Take Up Measure to Force Trump to End Iran War
summary: With four Republican backers, Democrats won a vote to advance a resolution that would force the president to end hostilities or win authorization from Congress.

title: Bond Yields Hit Highest Level Since 2007 as Inflation Fears Set In
summary: The 30-year U.S. Treasury yield hasn’t been this high since the lead-up to the global financial crisis. Across Europe and Asia, yields are also elevated.

title: Trump Crushes Republican Dissent: 8 Takeaways From Tuesday’s Primaries
summary: President Trump unseated Representative Thomas Massie, a top Republican critic in Congress, and also got his way in other primary contests.

title: I.R.S. to Drop Audits of Trump and Family
summary: As part of the Justice Department’s compensation fund deal, officials vowed not to pursue any matters, including those involving President Trump’s tax returns, that are pending.

title: Blanche Defends $1.8 Billion Fund as Skepticism Mounts
summary: The acting attorney general told lawmakers the fund would review claims from an array of people, not just Republicans.

title: Manager at Trump’s N.J. Golf Club Helped Plan Reflecting Pool Repairs
summary: David Schutzenhofer, a Trump Organization employee, reached out to one of the vendors the government later hired for the iconic site near the Lincoln Memorial, documents show.

title: Duffy Defends Road Trip Reality Show In Tense Senate Hearing
summary: Democrats called the transportation secretary’s reality-show-like series unethical.

title: ICE Makes Courthouse Arrest Despite Judge’s Order
summary: A Honduran man was taken into custody on Tuesday in New York City, which his lawyers said violated a prohibition imposed a day earlier. Hours later, the man was released.

title: The Best of ‘S.N.L.’ Season 51: Sensitive Strippers and Regretful Moms
summary: This season “Saturday Night Live” found new ways to satirize the Trump administration and said goodbye to one of its most valuable cast members.

title: Victims of San Diego Mosque Shooting Remembered as Heroes and Leaders
summary: The three men killed in the shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego were instrumental in delaying the attackers, the city’s police chief said.

title: The San Diego Mosque Shootings Were a Crime Made for and by the Internet
summary: Authorities said the teenagers who killed three men met online, posted their writings there and livestreamed the killings.

title: Trump Endorses Ken Paxton in Texas Senate Runoff Race Against John Cornyn
summary: The president embraced Ken Paxton, a MAGA loyalist, over Senator John Cornyn, despite warnings from Republican leaders about Mr. Paxton’s history of scandal.

title: Republican Senators Are Livid at Trump’s Endorsement of Paxton
summary: News that President Trump had snubbed Senator John Cornyn of Texas, the four-term incumbent, was met with shock, anger and fear that the G.O.P. could lose his seat.

title: Days After Hosting Trump, Xi Deepens Ties With Putin
summary: Meeting President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia in Beijing, China’s leader called for a halt to fighting in the Middle East and took a veiled swipe at the United States.

title: China Confirms It Will Buy 200 Boeing Planes
summary: The deal, which President Trump announced after last week’s summit, represents the largest single sale of the American company’s aircraft to Beijing in nearly a decade.

title: Woman Dies After Falling Into Open Manhole in Manhattan
summary: The woman plunged 10 feet, and a passer-by heard her screams. Con Edison said video footage suggested the manhole cover had been dislodged by a truck 12 minutes earlier.

title: Greg Hyman, Co-Creator of Tickle Me Elmo, Dies at 78
summary: An electronics wizard, he was already a veteran inventor when he collaborated with Ron Dubren on an idea for a toy that giggled. The rest is retail history.

title: Tickle Me Elmo and the ‘Elmo-Mania’ That Consumed America
summary: In 1996, the giggling stuffed doll turned holiday shopping into chaos, a preview of every sneaker drop, PlayStation launch and Taylor Swift ticket rush to come.

title: I’m the Foreign Minister of Sweden. Don’t Overestimate Russia.
summary: We can’t afford to underestimate Russia’s strength and its ability to harm Ukraine and NATO allies, but we must not overestimate it either.

title: Kentucky Had an Easy Choice Between Massie and Trump
summary: The president wants a loyal Republican Party — even if the country might not.

title: Late Night Could Set a Clock by Trump’s Iran Attack Delays
summary: Jimmy Kimmel noted that the president backs down on his own deadlines to bomb Iran “every Tuesday. It’s like a new episode of ‘NCIS.’”

title: Minnesota Becomes First State to Ban Prediction Markets
summary: Like at least other 14 states, Minnesota has wrestled with how to regulate the popular prediction market sites. Federal officials sued the state over its new law.

title: L.I.R.R. Workers Are in Line for Raises, but at What Cost to New York?
summary: Details are still emerging about the contract reached after a three-day strike that shut down America’s busiest passenger rail service. But the fallout could be felt for years.

title: Tornado Wipes Out Brand-New Home in Nebraska
summary: The tornado in Nebraska stayed on the ground for 11 minutes. A new subdivision called Dirt Road was wiped out, while nearby homes went untouched.

title: International Booker Prize 2026: ‘Taiwan Travelogue’ is First Winner Originally Written in Mandarin
summary: “Taiwan Travelogue” is the first novel originally written in Mandarin to win the major award for fiction translated into English.
